compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Implemented (replace, not add).

resolver.py:
- Removed the dead data.jira_key scan: _scan_alias_jira -> _scan_alias (alias-only, returns list[str]|None); dropped the ambiguous-jira_key error branch. The reconciler never writes data.jira_key (the live mapping is the binding store; jira_key only ever appears top-level on SYNC events, which the resolver never read), so that path was dead.
- Added _JIRA_KEY_RE (^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]+-[0-9]+$ — disjoint from lowercase-hex IDs/short-IDs and lowercase-word aliases) and _resolve_via_binding_store(target, tracker_dir), which reads <tracker>/.bridge_state/bindings.json reverse index, tries the key verbatim then upper-cased, and returns the local dir iff it exists. Missing/corrupt store, non-dict reverse, unbound key, or stale binding -> None (never raises).
- resolve_ticket_id consults the binding store for Jira-shaped input after the short-id block, before the alias scan. Updated module + function docstrings.

Tests (test_ticket_alias_backfill.py): replaced the jira_key-collision test with binding-store coverage (bound resolves; lowercase input upper-cased; unbound -> None; missing bindings.json -> None; corrupt bindings.json -> None; binding -> missing dir -> None) plus a regression that _scan_alias no longer buckets jira_key fields. Alias/short/full/prefix + hard-failure-diagnostic coverage kept green.

Verification:
- module: 20 passed.
- reducer+graph+bridge suites: 305 passed, 1 skipped.
- ruff check + format-check: clean.
- End-to-end via repo build: resolve_ticket_id('REB-310') -> c8cc-68b8-a51c-47cd; 'reb-310' -> same (upper-cased); 'REB-9999' -> None; alias 'crude-skip-swoop' -> 00da-...; 'rebar show REB-310' resolves and renders the ticket.

Note: the global 'rebar' CLI in PATH is a stale build without the [agents] extra (no review-plan subcommand), so the LLM plan-review/completion gates aren't runnable here; verified through the .venv repo build instead.

compiled_state.description:
## What

The centralized resolver `rebar._engine_support.resolver.resolve_ticket_id` is the single seam used by the library (`__init__.py`), `signing.py`, all `_commands`, `_engine_support` reads, the reducer link-resolution path, and plan-review. It contains a DEAD Jira-resolution path: `_scan_alias_jira` matches `data.jira_key` on CREATE/SNAPSHOT events, but that field is never written (the reconciler stores the mapping in the binding store; `jira_key` only ever appears as a top-level field on SYNC events, which the resolver does not read). The lookups.py docstring already documents `data.jira_key` as "intentionally not reflected". The authoritative Jira<->rebar mapping is the binding store reverse index at `.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json` (`reverse: {jira_key -> local_id}`).

## Why

`rebar show REB-310` (and claim/transition/link/library) cannot resolve a Jira key today even though the binding exists. Per user direction: REPLACE the unused `data.jira_key` resolution path with binding-store-backed resolution, rather than adding a second mechanism beside dead code — to avoid future confusion.

## Approach (replace, not add)

In `src/rebar/_engine_support/resolver.py`:
- REMOVE the `data.jira_key` matching from `_scan_alias_jira` (it becomes alias-only; rename to `_scan_alias`, return `list[str] | None`). Drop the ambiguous-jira_key error branch in `resolve_ticket_id`.
- ADD `_JIRA_KEY_RE = ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]+-[0-9]+$` (disjoint from lowercase-hex IDs/short-IDs and lowercase-word aliases) and a best-effort `_resolve_via_binding_store(target, tracker_dir)` that reads `<tracker_dir>/.bridge_state/bindings.json`, looks up `reverse[target]` (and `target.upper()`), and returns the local ticket-dir name iff it exists on disk; missing/corrupt store or non-dict reverse -> None (never raises).
- In `resolve_ticket_id`, for Jira-key-shaped input consult the binding store (after the short-id block, before the alias scan). Update the module + function docstrings to describe binding-store resolution.

## Scope

Resolution only (input -> ticket). The `lookups.py::_auto` DISPLAY path also reads the dead `data.jira_key`; left as-is (documented fallback chain, not resolution) — capture as follow-up if we want auto-display of Jira keys.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `resolve_ticket_id("REB-310", tracker)` returns the bound local ticket dir when `.bridge_state/bindings.json` reverse-maps it and the dir exists.
- [ ] The dead `data.jira_key` scan is removed: `_scan_alias` no longer reads/returns jira matches; no `data.jira_key` resolution remains in resolver.py.
- [ ] Jira-shaped input with no binding (or missing/corrupt bindings.json, or binding to a non-existent dir) returns None without raising and without false alias/prefix match.
- [ ] Existing alias / short-id / full-id / prefix resolution unchanged (no regression); hard tracker-listing failure still returns None + a stderr diagnostic.
- [ ] Tests updated: replace the old jira_key-collision test with binding-store coverage (bound -> resolves; unbound -> None; missing/corrupt store -> None; binding -> non-existent dir -> None) and keep alias coverage green.
- [ ] `rebar show REB-310` resolves end-to-end (manual check noted on the ticket).
- [ ] make lint + the resolver test module pass.
compiled_state.title: Resolve Jira issue keys (REB-NNN) in the centralized ID resolver via the binding store
